Location: La Loma Catholic Cemetery, Caloocan City, Metro Manila
Remarks: A feeding flock of Pycnonotus goiavier amongst a tall Eucalyptus deglupta (?). The flock is augmented by birds coming from a nearby tree as others leave the feeding tree. Song of Rhipidura nigrotorquis and Geopelia striata can also be heard.

This was recorded at 6:04 am. The bird sings everyday from the crown of the tree ( it is approximately the height of the roof of a two story house. The tree is in the middle of 3 houses, a few meters from the front yard with 5 mature trees.

The immature bird is begging for the parent bird to feed it, with rapid flapping of wings from its perch. The immature bird is only slightly smaller than the parent bird and plumage is almost like that of the adult.  The tree branch is about 15 feet from the ground.
Sound of traffick can be heard in the background.

Location: La Loma Catholic Cemetery, Caloocan City, Metro Manila
Remarks: Bird is calling from the crown of a small tree located in the middle of a grassy land. Bird is preenings in-between calls.

Location: La Loma Catholic Cemetery, Caloocan City, Metro Manila
Remarks: Bird is calling from the crown of a small tree located in the middle of a grassy land.
Two birds are calling from the crown of a Chrysophyllum cainito. These are two of the various calls made by this bird.   One of the songs of the Pycnonotus goiavier can be heard at 0:35.
